findings: Hook (babysitting story: 5h + 10 = 45) gives the student a concrete reason to care about isolating a variable before any formal vocabulary lands.
Balance slide explicitly states the only legal move — same operation, both sides — which is exactly the rule students forget when they 'move the 7 over' without naming what they did.

Trap slide (08) disarms the single most common two-step error: dividing by the coefficient before stripping the constant, which produces fraction soup. Both wrong-path and right-path arithmetic are shown side by side.
Pause #1 and Pause #2 both demand a substitution check, mirroring the assignment rubric. Answer-reveal slides label every move (add 7 / divide by 2 / verify) so students can self-grade.

Final path slide ends with a concrete Learn Portal next action: read OpenStax 2.1–2.2, run Khan Academy two-step practice, complete the dashboard's 15-equation + 5-verification + 1-word-problem assignment.
watch_points: Slide 06 (two-step trace) and slide 11 (assignment box) are visually denser than the others; keep narration pace measured during recording so the student can read along.
Pause #2 uses negatives and a divisor; encourage advisors to flag a student who can solve it but cannot verbalize why subtracting 3 first preserves the equation.
